title: SSO login redirect loop for users whose email in Entra ID differs from stored TaskFlow email (plus-addressing and aliased accounts)
problem: After migrating from Okta to Microsoft Entra ID for SSO, approximately 30% of users experience an infinite redirect loop when logging into TaskFlow. The user authenticates successfully with Entra ID, is redirected back to TaskFlow, and is immediately sent back to Entra ID again. Affected users correlate strongly with those who use plus-addressed emails (e.g., jane+taskflow@company.com) or whose email addresses changed during the migration.
root_cause_hypothesis: Two interacting issues: (1) **Email claim mismatch**: Okta sent the full plus-addressed email (jane+taskflow@company.com) as the identity claim, but Entra ID sends only the base email (jane@company.com). Similarly, migrated/aliased users have a new email in Entra but their old email in TaskFlow. When TaskFlow receives the SSO callback, it tries to match the token's email claim against its user records and fails. (2) **Missing error handling on user-not-found**: Instead of showing an error when no matching user is found after SSO callback, TaskFlow appears to redirect back to the login page, which re-initiates the SSO flow, creating the infinite loop. A secondary issue is that stale Okta session cookies may interfere with the Entra flow for some users, explaining why clearing cookies or using incognito sometimes resolves it.
reproduction_steps: Have a user account in TaskFlow stored with a plus-addressed email (e.g., jane+taskflow@company.com) or a pre-migration email alias
Ensure the corresponding Entra ID profile has the base email (jane@company.com) or the new aliased email
Attempt to log into TaskFlow via SSO
Observe: user authenticates successfully with Entra ID, is redirected to TaskFlow, then immediately redirected back to Entra ID in a loop
Optionally: observe that the login page UI flashes briefly before each redirect
environment: TaskFlow with SSO authentication, recently migrated from Okta to Microsoft Entra ID. Entra ID app registration and redirect URIs have been verified as correct. Affects ~30% of users, cross-cutting across teams and roles.
impact: ~30% of users are completely unable to log into TaskFlow through normal means. Workarounds (clearing cookies, incognito) are unreliable and not viable long-term.
recommended_fix: Two changes needed: (1) **Fix email matching logic**: Update the user lookup that runs after SSO callback to handle email normalization. Options include stripping the plus-address suffix before lookup, matching on a canonical email field, or performing a case-insensitive/alias-aware lookup. For migrated users, either update their stored emails in TaskFlow to match Entra ID, or add support for multiple email aliases per account. (2) **Fix the redirect loop**: Add proper error handling for the case where SSO authentication succeeds but no matching TaskFlow user is found — display a clear error message (e.g., 'No account found for this email, contact your admin') instead of redirecting back to login. (3) **Optional**: Invalidate or clear stale Okta session cookies during the migration period to prevent the secondary cookie-interference issue.
proposed_test_case: Create a test user in TaskFlow with a plus-addressed email (user+tag@example.com). Configure the SSO mock/stub to return the base email (user@example.com) in the identity token. Verify that: (a) the user is successfully matched and logged in after the email matching fix, and (b) if matching still fails for any reason, an error page is shown instead of a redirect loop.
information_gaps: Exact code path in TaskFlow's SSO callback handler that performs user lookup and the specific field/claim used for matching
Whether TaskFlow uses the 'email' or 'preferred_username' claim from the OIDC token
Whether there is an existing email alias or secondary email field in TaskFlow's user model that could be leveraged
